evals.prompt: I want to fine-tune Qwen3 on my own chat dataset on Modal. Use a single GPU, make it resumable if the run gets interrupted, and keep costs reasonable.
evals.expected_output: A working plan or code sketch for a single-node Unsloth or PEFT-style SFT or QLoRA workflow on Modal that caches the base model, stores checkpoints in Volumes, uses Secrets for external credentials, and exposes the important hyperparameters via a local entrypoint or CLI flags.
expectations: The response chooses an LLM SFT or LoRA workflow rather than diffusion, YOLO, RL, or inference serving
The response prefers LoRA or QLoRA over full fine-tuning unless the user states otherwise
The response uses Modal Volumes for model cache and checkpoints
The response accounts for resumable checkpoints or retry safety
The response mentions Modal Secrets for Hugging Face, Weights & Biases, or other external credentials when relevant
The response keeps the design single-node and GPU-aware
The response does not drift into OpenAI-compatible serving guidance

evals.prompt: I have 12 product photos and want to fine-tune a text-to-image model on Modal so I can generate more images in the same style. Save outputs I can inspect after training.
evals.expected_output: A Diffusers LoRA fine-tuning workflow on Modal that stages the image dataset, uses a Volume for checkpoints and sample outputs, calls out any gated model access or secrets requirements, and keeps training and later serving concerns separated.
expectations: The response chooses a diffusion LoRA workflow rather than an LLM or YOLO workflow
The response stores checkpoints and sample outputs in a Modal Volume
The response mentions any gated Hugging Face model access and Secret requirements when relevant
The response keeps the implementation single-node and GPU-aware
The response separates training from later inference or web serving
The response does not recommend generic batch orchestration as the primary pattern

evals.prompt: Help me fine-tune YOLO on Modal for a labeled image dataset. I need a path that can mount or download the dataset, train on GPUs, and keep the training artifacts around for later evaluation.
evals.expected_output: A YOLO-style vision fine-tuning workflow on Modal that stages or downloads the dataset, uses a Volume for dataset persistence and training outputs, identifies required secrets such as Roboflow when relevant, and keeps the scope on training rather than streaming inference.
expectations: The response chooses the YOLO workflow rather than diffusion, LLM SFT, or RL
The response handles dataset staging through a Modal Volume or an equivalent persisted path
The response persists training artifacts for later inspection
The response mentions Secrets when the dataset provider requires them
The response keeps the design single-node and GPU-aware
The response does not drift into WebRTC or production inference architecture

evals.prompt: I want to do GRPO post-training for an LLM on Modal. Pick the right framework for me and explain how to organize checkpoints, rewards, and GPU usage.
evals.expected_output: A GRPO training design that picks TRL for the simpler single-node trainer path unless the task needs the more explicit verl split, persists checkpoints in Volumes, uses Secrets for logging or model access, and explains when sandbox-backed reward execution is appropriate.
expectations: The response chooses GRPO as an RL post-training workflow and distinguishes it from supervised fine-tuning
The response explains the tradeoff between TRL and verl and picks one for the described task
The response persists checkpoints in a Modal Volume
The response mentions Secrets for Hugging Face, Weights & Biases, or similar systems when relevant
The response is GPU-aware and stays single-node by default
The response treats sandbox usage as a reward-execution detail rather than the main workflow
The response does not turn into guidance for deploying an inference API
